Correct answer: A
Solution

• 2NF can’t have partial functional dependencies.
• 3NF can’t have transitive dependencies.
• 4NF deals with multi-valued dependencies.
• 5NF deals with join dependencies.

Q4 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : SQL’s aggregation Sum, Avg, Count can not be expressed in relational algebra.
S2 : Select clause with distinct in SQL is same as project in relational algebra.
S3 : In SQL the arithmetic operator (+, –, *, ÷,) all return null if one of the their arguments is null.
Which of the above statement is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
S1 : SQL’s aggregation can not be expressed in relational algebra.
S2 : Select clause is same as project in relation algebra.
S3 : Arithmetic operation is return null if one of the value is null.
All S1, S2 and S3 are correct.

Q5 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If every non-key attribute is functionally dependent on the primary key, then the relation will always be in?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(c)
If non-key attribute functionally dependent on the primary key then the relation will always be in the 3NF. However it may not in the BCNF because there might be the prime attribute functionally dependent on some attribute other than primary key.

Q6 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are false for relational DBMS?
I. ER diagrams are useful to logically model concepts.
II. Views in a database system are important because they help with access control by allowing users to see only a particular subset of the data in the database.
III. SQL is a procedural language.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
I. A conceptual ER model may be used as the foundation for one as more logical data models.
III. SQL is not procedural language.
II is correct statement.

Q8 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following relations:

R1 has 500 records and R2 has 800 records. The non-Null attribute ‘P ’ in R2 is referencing attribute
‘P’ in R1. The maximum number of records present in are ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 800
Solution

800
Since ‘P ’ in R2 is not key, hence all value of ‘P’ may or may not be unique. Hence every entry under ‘P’ in R2 will match with ‘P’ in R1. Hence maximum is 800.

Q9 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the schedules S1 and S2 given below and the statements given:

Which of the following options is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• In S1, T2 reads item Y from T3 but T2 commits before T3 commits. So S1 is non-recoverable, cascade problem and not strict recoverable.
• S2 is not strict because T2 writes before T3 commits but S2 is cascadeless because there is no transaction reads item that were written by an uncommitted transaction.
• Option (b) and (d) are correct.

Consider the following statements:
I. All attribute of a relation form a super key.
II. Super key is also a candidate key.
III. For any SQL query there exists a unique translation into relational algebra.
Which of the above statement is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
All attribute of a relation form a super key.
Candidate key is also super key not vice-versa.
For any SQL query there exists one or more than one translation into relational algebra.

Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is false?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(c)
For a conflict serializable schedule, the linear ordering of transactions corresponds to topological sorting of the serialization graph. Since there can be multiple such orderings, multiple equivalent serial schedules may exist.

Q31 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• A record at all transaction and the corresponding changes to the database is recorded a log.
• 2PL ensure conflict serializability and also suffer from starvation.
• Conservative 2PL free form the deadlock.
• Time-stamp concurrency control algorithm is a non-lock concurrency control method. In timestamp based method, deadlock cannot occur as no transaction ever waits.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Given relations A(a, b) and B(c, d), the result of SELECT DISTINCT a, b from A, B is guaranteed to be same as A if

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
For the output to be same as relation A two conditions must be satisfied.
(i) A has no duplicates: If A has duplicates then output will not contain duplicates because of DISTINCT keyword (output will not be same as A(a, b)).
(ii) B is non-empty: If B(c, d) is empty then cross product of A and B will result empty rows, so is the output (output will not be same as A(a, b)).
